Interface ReactiveValueOperations<K,V>

public interface ReactiveValueOperations<K,V>
Reactive Redis operations for simple (or in Redis terminology 'string') values.

Streams of methods returning Mono<K> or Flux<M> are terminated with InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException when RedisElementReader.read(ByteBuffer) returns null for a particular element as Reactive Streams prohibit the usage of null values.

Since:
2.0
Author:
Mark Paluch, Jiahe Cai, Yordan Tsintsov

    Append a value to key.

    Get / Manipulate specific integer fields of varying bit widths and arbitrary non (necessary) aligned offset stored at a given key.

    Compare the value at key with expectedValue and set it to newValue if they are equal.

    Get multiple keys.

    Set multiple keys to multiple values using key-value pairs provided in tuple.
    Mono<Boolean>

    Set multiple keys to multiple values using key-value pairs provided in tuple only if the provided key does not exist.

  • Method Details

    • set

      Mono<Boolean> set(K key, V value)
      Set value for key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

    • set

      Mono<Boolean> set(K key, V value, Expiration expiration)
      Set value for key with expiration.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      expiration - must not be null.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

    • set

      default Mono<Boolean> set(K key, V value, Duration timeout)
      Set the value and expiration timeout for key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value -
      timeout - must not be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

    • set

      Mono<Boolean> set(K key, V value, Consumer<SetSpec<K,V>> spec)
      Set value for key and customize the operation through SetSpec.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      spec - a function that consumes the SetSpec to configure the set operation, must not be null.
      Returns:
      true if the operation was successful, false otherwise.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

    • setGet

      Mono<V> setGet(K key, V value, Expiration expiration)
      Set the value and expiration for key. Return the old string stored at key, or empty if key did not exist. An error is returned and SET aborted if the value stored at key is not a string.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      expiration - must not be null.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

    • setGet

      default Mono<V> setGet(K key, V value, Duration timeout)
      Set the value and expiration timeout for key. Return the old string stored at key, or empty if key did not exist. An error is returned and SET aborted if the value stored at key is not a string.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value -
      timeout - must not be null.
      Since:
      3.5
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SETEX

    • setGet

      Mono<V> setGet(K key, V value, Consumer<SetSpec<K,V>> spec)
      Set value for key and customize the operation through SetSpec. Return the old string stored at key, or null if key did not exist.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      spec - a function that consumes the SetSpec to configure the set operation, must not be null.
      Returns:
      the old value stored at key, or null if key did not exist.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

    • setIfAbsent

      default Mono<Boolean> setIfAbsent(K key, V value)
      Set key to hold the string value if key is absent.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value -
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      expiration - must not be null.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

      key - must not be null.
      value -
      timeout - must not be null.
      Since:

      Set key to hold the string value if key is present.

      key - must not be null.
      value -

      Mono<Boolean> setIfPresent(K key, V value, Expiration expiration)

      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      expiration - must not be null.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:

      default Mono<Boolean> setIfPresent(K key, V value, Duration timeout)
      Set key to hold the string value and expiration timeout if key is present.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value -
      timeout - must not be null.
      Since:
      2.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

    • compareAndSet

      Mono<Boolean> compareAndSet(K key, V expectedValue, V newValue)
      Compare the value at key with expectedValue and set it to newValue if they are equal. Use set(Object, Object, Consumer) to customize the set operation using e.g. a different value comparison strategy.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      expectedValue - the expected current value, must not be null.
      newValue - the new value to set if comparison succeeds, must not be null.
      Returns:
      true if the operation was successful, false otherwise.
      Since:
      4.1
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SET

    • multiSet

      Mono<Boolean> multiSet(Map<? extends K, ? extends V> map)
      Set multiple keys to multiple values using key-value pairs provided in tuple.
      Parameters:
      map - must not be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: MSET

    • multiSetIfAbsent

      Mono<Boolean> multiSetIfAbsent(Map<? extends K, ? extends V> map)
      Set multiple keys to multiple values using key-value pairs provided in tuple only if the provided key does not exist.
      Parameters:
      map - must not be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: MSETNX

    • get

      Mono<V> get(Object key)
      Get the value of key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.

      • Redis Documentation: GET

      Mono<V> getAndDelete(K key)
      Return the value at key and delete the key.

      key - must not be null.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: GETDEL

    • getAndExpire

      Mono<V> getAndExpire(K key, Duration timeout)
      Return the value at key and expire the key by applying timeout.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      timeout - must not be null.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: GETEX

    • getAndPersist

      Mono<V> getAndPersist(K key)
      Return the value at key and persist the key. This operation removes any TTL that is associated with key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: GETEX

    • getAndSet

      Mono<V> getAndSet(K key, V value)
      Set value of key and return its old value.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: GETSET

    • multiGet

      Mono<List<V>> multiGet(Collection<K> keys)
      Get multiple keys. Values are in the order of the requested keys. Absent field values are represented using null in the resulting List.
      Parameters:
      keys - must not be null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: MGET
